problem text: Suppose $X, Y, Z \sim N(12, 12)$ and are IID. Determine $\mathbb{P}[X-Y > Z]$. The solution is expressed as $\Phi(a)$ for some value $a$. Identify $a$.
problem solution: Express this as $\mathbb{P}[X-Y-Z > 0]$. The random variable $W = X - Y - Z \sim N(-12, 36)$ due to the characteristics of independent normal variables. Hence, we have $\mathbb{P}[W > 0]$, which can be standardized to $\mathbb{P}\left[\dfrac{W+12}{6} > \dfrac{12}{6}\right]$. The left-hand side is now standard normal, leading to $\Phi(-2)$ by the symmetry property of the normal distribution. Therefore, $a = -2$.
problem hint: Reformulate this as $\mathbb{P}[X-Y-Z > 0]$. What is the probability distribution of $X - Y - Z$?
